key_points: India became independent from British rule on 15 August 1947
The Constitution of India came into effect on 26 January 1950
The Constituent Assembly drafted the Constitution
India reorganized states mainly on linguistic basis in 1956
Five-Year Plans were introduced to guide economic development
The Green Revolution increased food production in India
India follows democracy where citizens elect their representatives
Unity in diversity is an important feature of Indian society
examples: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee
Andhra Pradesh was the first state created on linguistic basis in 1953
Punjab and Haryana benefited greatly from the Green Revolution
ISRO was established to develop space technology in India
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel integrated princely states into India
important_terms: Constitution – The set of fundamental rules and laws that govern a country
Democracy – A system of government where people elect their leaders
Linguistic States – States formed based on language spoken by people
Five-Year Plan – Government plan for economic development for five years
Green Revolution – Agricultural program that increased food production using modern technology
exam_tips: Remember important dates such as 15 August 1947 and 26 January 1950
Learn key leaders involved in nation building like Dr. B. R. Ambedkar and Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
Understand the purpose of Five-Year Plans and the Green Revolution
Write clear definitions for terms like democracy, constitution, and national integration
Use examples while explaining development after independence
